title: The Fight Is Not Over
description: On pages 17–18, this part focuses on the fight is not over, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Fight Is Not Over. Luffy survives his first loss to Crocodile and begins the long road back into the fight for Alabasta. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Before the battle begins at the Eastern gates.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Mean- while at the Western Gates...

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Where to lie in wait for them...

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: They've already beaten 6 of our numbered officer agents...

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: Yes, indeedio! And princess Vivi Aersee!! was a former Baroque Works, agent too!

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: In the art of deception, J cannot be beaten!

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: ...Well, we can always crush them in one stroke if we felt like it.

summary: This part develops the fight is not over and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
